title: Good Friday Bloodshed: 8 Murdered in Culiacán — 34th Municipal Officer Killed Since September 2024
detail: Eight people were murdered in Culiacán on Good Friday, April 4, 2026, making Sinaloa the deadliest state in Mexico that day with 7 classified intentional homicides — contributing to a national total of 64. Municipal police officer Jose Luis, a 4-year veteran, was shot multiple times while riding his motorcycle on the Benito Juárez bypass ('La Costerita'), Punta Azul sector. His death was classified separately as 'aggression against a public security officer' rather than a standard homicide, bringing the cumulative total of Culiacán municipal officers killed since September 2024 to 34. Violence persisted despite a Semana Santa SEDENA/SEMAR security surge. Local journalists attribute the killings to ongoing criminal disputes between Chapitos and Mayitos factions.
